mechanism: GA binds the TaGID1 receptor pocket inducing conformational change that permits DELLA interaction; homeolog-independent.
evidence_sentence: Binding of bioactive GAs within a pocket in the GID1 protein induces a conformational change that permits interaction between the DELLA and GID1 proteins

mechanism: GA-bound TaGID1 binds DELLA N-terminal DELLA/TVHYNP motif via closed N-terminal lid, recruiting SCF-SLY1 E3 ligase for DELLA degradation.
evidence_sentence: This leads to closing of the N-terminal lid of GID1, allowing DELLAs to bind through their conserved N-terminal DELLA/TVHYNP motifs

mechanism: GA-bound TaGID1 targets Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog for proteasomal degradation via SCF-SLY1.
evidence_sentence: Strong interactions between each putative TaGID1 and the wheat DELLA proteins RHT-A1, RHT-B1, and RHT-D1 in the presence of GA3

mechanism: SCF-SLY1/GID2 F-box ubiquitinates GA-GID1-DELLA complex, targeting Rht-B1 DELLA for 26S proteasomal degradation.
evidence_sentence: GID1/DELLA binding enables recognition by the GID2/SLEEPY1 F-box component of an SCF Ub E3 ligase, leading to ubiquitination of DELLAs and their subsequent 26S proteasome-mediated degradation

mechanism: SCF-SLY1 ubiquitinates Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog (parallel to Rht-B1 mechanism).
evidence_sentence: GID1/DELLA binding enables recognition by the GID2/SLEEPY1 F-box component of an SCF Ub E3 ligase, leading to ubiquitination of DELLAs

mechanism: Rht-B1 DELLA represses GA-responsive growth; Rht-B1b semi-dwarf allele produces ~20% height reduction; Rht-B1c extreme allele ~50% reduction.
evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins, which act to repress GA-responsive growth, and their mutant alleles Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b are thought to confer dwarfism by producing more active forms of these growth repressors

mechanism: Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog represses GA-responsive growth; Rht-D1b semi-dwarf produces ~20% reduction; Rht-D1c extreme ~50% via gene amplification.
evidence_sentence: Rht-B1 and Rht-D1 encode DELLA proteins, which act to repress GA-responsive growth

mechanism: GA 20-oxidase catalyses GA12->GA9 and GA53->GA20; rate-limiting step for bioactive GA; highest expression at elongating nodes.
evidence_sentence: GA 20-oxidase and 3-oxidase are major sites of regulation in GA biosynthesis; enzymes catalyze multi-step reactions, including conversions of GA12-GA9, and GA53-GA20

mechanism: GA 3-oxidase catalyses 3β-hydroxylation of GA20->GA1 (bioactive); TaGA3ox2 essential for wheat growth; homeologs GA3ox2 A1/B1/D1.
evidence_sentence: GA3OX3 catalyses the 3β-hydroxylation of GA20 to GA1

mechanism: GA 2-oxidase catabolises bioactive GA1/GA4 and precursor GA12 to inactive GA8/GA34/GA110; depletes bioactive GA pool.
evidence_sentence: increased expression of GA2oxA9 in Rht18 results in a reduction of both bioactive GA content and plant height

mechanism: TaGA1ox1-B1 1β-hydroxylates GA9->GA61 and GA20->GA60, diverting precursors from bioactive GA pool; loss increases bioactive GA.
evidence_sentence: ga1ox1 Mutant: Bioactive GA1/GA4 extremely high; 10.7 cm taller in 2021 field trial

mechanism: GA induces rapid Rht-B1 DELLA degradation via GID1-SCF-SLY1; relieves growth repression.
evidence_sentence: Bioactive GAs relieve the DELLA growth repression by targeting their rapid degradation through a GID1-GA receptor-mediated signalling pathway

mechanism: GA targets Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog for degradation (parallel to Rht-B1).
evidence_sentence: Bioactive GAs relieve the DELLA growth repression by targeting their rapid degradation through a GID1-GA receptor-mediated signalling pathway

mechanism: Negative GA feedback: exogenous GA3 represses TaGA20ox-A2/B2 transcripts.
evidence_sentence: exogenous GA3 reduces the expressions of GA20oxs and GA3oxs, and increases the expressions of GA2oxs

mechanism: Negative feedback: GA3 decreases TaGA3ox-B2/D2 transcripts.
evidence_sentence: exogenous GA3 reduces the expressions of GA20oxs and GA3oxs

mechanism: GA homeostatic feedforward: GA3 increases TaGA2ox expression, sustaining catabolism of excess GA.
evidence_sentence: exogenous GA3 increases the expressions of GA2oxs

mechanism: Negative feedback on TaGID1: exogenous GA3 down-regulates TaGID1-A1/B1/D1 transcripts.
evidence_sentence: down-regulated after treatment with exogenous gibberellic acid (GA3), indicating feedback control

mechanism: DELLA promotes GA20ox transcription as part of homeostatic feedback (when DELLA accumulates, GA biosynthesis upregulates).
evidence_sentence: DELLA protein signaling alters GA oxidase gene transcription through GA-GID1-DELLA tricomplex

mechanism: PLATZ-A1 (Rht25) loss-of-function reduces plant height 12.7% and peduncle length 18.7%; overexpression increases height; causal Rht25 gene.
evidence_sentence: Natural and induced loss-of-function mutations in PLATZ-A1 reduce plant height and its overexpression increases plant height, demonstrating that PLATZ-A1 is the causative gene of RHT25

mechanism: DELLA GRAS PFYRE subdomain physically binds PLATZ-A1, sequestering it; genetic interaction confirmed on height (P=0.011).
evidence_sentence: PLATZ-A1 showed a positive interaction with RHT1-GRAS in yeast-two-hybrid assays

mechanism: PLATZ-A1 promotes GRF1/GRF5/CYCD4;1 expression; loss-of-function causes 17-55% downregulation.
evidence_sentence: Loss-of-function caused downregulation of GRF1, GRF5, CYCD4;1 (-17 to -55% reduction)

mechanism: GRF4 physically interacts with DELLA; competes for DELLA binding with PLATZ1 and antagonises DELLA growth repression.
evidence_sentence: The balanced opposing activities and physical interactions of the GROWTH-REGULATING FACTOR 4 (GRF4) transcription factor and the growth inhibitor DELLA confer homeostatic co-regulation of growth

mechanism: GRF4 promotes growth, N-assimilation, carbon fixation; OE in Rht-B1b background boosts yield without additional height.
evidence_sentence: GRF4 promotes and integrates nitrogen assimilation, carbon fixation and growth, whereas DELLA inhibits these processes

mechanism: BR ligand binds TaBRI1 extracellular island domain, activating receptor kinase; triggers BSK-CDG1-BSU1 cascade.
evidence_sentence: BR hormones regulate critical developmental traits in wheat through the BRI1 receptor

mechanism: Activated TaBRI1 facilitates ZnF-mediated ubiquitination of TaBKI1 at plasma membrane, releasing receptor inhibition.
evidence_sentence: TaBRI1 facilitates the ZnF-TaBKI1 conjugation in response to BR signalling

mechanism: TaZnF RING-type E3 ligase ubiquitinates TaBKI1 for proteasomal destruction; BR-dependent activator of signalling.
evidence_sentence: ZnF acts as a BR signalling activator to facilitate proteasomal destruction of the BR signalling repressor BRI1 kinase inhibitor 1 (TaBKI1)

mechanism: TaBKI1 inhibits TaBRI1 kinase activity in absence of BR; removed upon BR binding.
evidence_sentence: BR signalling repressor BRI1 kinase inhibitor 1 (TaBKI1)

mechanism: Activated BRI1 via BSK1/CDG1/BSU1 phosphatase inactivates TaBIN2 GSK3-like kinase.
evidence_sentence: In BR signaling, transphosphorylation events mediated through BSK1, CDG1 and BIN2 kinases lead to the activation of BSU1 phosphatase, which acts to dephosphorylate and inactivate BIN2, the negative regulator of BR signaling

mechanism: BIN2 phosphorylates BZR1/BES1, inhibiting nuclear localisation and DNA binding; active when BR absent.
evidence_sentence: At low BR concentrations, BIN2 phosphorylates two homologous transcription factors, BZR1 and BES1/BZR2, and inhibits their nuclear localization and DNA-binding activity

mechanism: Dephosphorylated TaBZR1/TaBES1 binds DNA of growth/cell-elongation genes, promoting stem elongation.
evidence_sentence: The dephosphorylated active transcription factors, BZR1 and BES1/BZR2, can then bind to specific sites on DNA and regulate expression of several genes

mechanism: Functional TaBRI1 promotes stem elongation; triple mutants severely dwarfed; single missense reduces height 35%.
evidence_sentence: Triple mutants (with premature stop codons in all three TaBRI1 homoeologues) produced severe dwarfism, malformed leaves and sterility

mechanism: BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA; BR-GA crosstalk; BZR1 sequesters DELLA and vice-versa (antagonistic).
evidence_sentence: BRs and GAs co-regulate a large number of common target genes and BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA proteins

mechanism: BIN2 phosphorylates DELLA, stabilising it (BR-GA crosstalk); increased when BR low; positive regulator of DELLA accumulation.
evidence_sentence: BRs and GAs co-regulate a large number of common target genes and BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA proteins; Deletions of domains required for BZR1-DELLA interaction abolished the antagonistic effect

mechanism: TaD11 (BR C6-oxidase ortholog) catalyses BR biosynthesis step; tad11-2a reduces endogenous BR and causes dwarfism.
evidence_sentence: The tad11-2a-1 mutant displayed dwarfism, smaller grains, sensitivity to 24-epiBL, and reduced endogenous BR contents

mechanism: Negative BR feedback: exogenous 24-epiBL suppresses TaD11 transcripts.
evidence_sentence: TaD11 genes were highly expressed in roots (Zadoks scale: Z11) and grains (Z75), while expression was significantly suppressed by exogenous BR (24-epiBL)

mechanism: Auxin binds TIR1/AFB F-box forming co-receptor with Aux/IAA substrate.
evidence_sentence: When present, auxin promotes the TIR1/AFB-mediated ubiquitin-proteasome-dependent degradation of inhibitory Aux/IAA proteins

mechanism: Auxin-loaded TIR1 targets Aux/IAA proteins for proteasomal degradation.
evidence_sentence: TIR1/AFB-mediated ubiquitin-proteasome-dependent degradation of inhibitory Aux/IAA proteins

mechanism: Aux/IAA binds and represses ARF transcription factors; removal de-represses ARFs.
evidence_sentence: thereby activating ARF transcription factors

mechanism: TaARF (ARF4, ARF9, etc.) activate growth/elongation genes; TaARF4-B haplotypes associate with PH QTL.
evidence_sentence: TaARF4-B was strongly associated with PH and root depth at the tillering, jointing and grain fill stages

mechanism: Auxin induces expansin expression; expansins loosen cell walls for elongation.
evidence_sentence: Expansin expression is influenced by hormones like abscisic acid, indole-3-acetic acid (auxins), brassinosteroids, cytokinins and ethylene

mechanism: GA promotes expansin expression to drive stem elongation.
evidence_sentence: expansin expression is influenced by hormones like abscisic acid, indole-3-acetic acid (auxins), brassinosteroids, cytokinins and ethylene

mechanism: BR induces expansin expression in elongating tissues.
evidence_sentence: expansin expression is influenced by hormones like abscisic acid, indole-3-acetic acid (auxins), brassinosteroids, cytokinins and ethylene

mechanism: Expansins disrupt H-bonds between cellulose and xyloglucan, allowing cell wall creep and internode elongation.
evidence_sentence: plants carrying a single copy of the OsEXP4 gene grew taller than control plants and developed some additional leaves

mechanism: VRN1 binds TaGA20ox2 promoter and activates GA biosynthesis after vernalization; coordinates heading and height.
evidence_sentence: VRN1 binds to and activates the TaGA20ox2 promoter

mechanism: VRN1-5A/5B/5D redundantly regulate plant height (via GA biosynthesis); triple KO dwarfs plants.
evidence_sentence: VRN1-5A/5B/5D redundantly regulate heading date and plant height in wheat; the plant height of these mutants was reduced by 3.7-5 cm

mechanism: Cold exposure (vernalization) epigenetically induces VRN1 expression in shoot apex.
evidence_sentence: VRN1 regulates heading and plant height in wheat by activating gibberellin biosynthesis

mechanism: Rht8 = TaRNHL-D1 (Ribonuclease H-like); loss-of-function reduces internode length via reduced cell elongation; GA-sensitive pathway.
evidence_sentence: The map-based cloning of Rht8 confirmed that loss of Ribonuclease H-Like 1 (RNHL-D1) is responsible for Rht8 semi-dwarfing effect

mechanism: Rht8/TaRNHL-D1 promotes GA biosynthesis in stem; loss reduces bioactive GA.
evidence_sentence: controls the plant height and spike length via GA biosynthesis

mechanism: Autoactive NB-LRR (Rht-B13b S240F) upregulates PR genes including class III peroxidases.
evidence_sentence: autoactivation of Rht13 leads to upregulation of pathogenesis-related (PR) genes, including class III peroxidases

mechanism: Class III peroxidases catalyse cell-wall cross-linking, limiting cell elongation and stem growth.
evidence_sentence: class III peroxidases, which may catalyse the cross-linking of cell wall compounds to limit cell elongation and hence reduce height

mechanism: Rht-B1b NIL reduces flag leaf photosynthetic rate 18% and chlorophyll A 23%.
evidence_sentence: at anthesis, Rht-B1b reduces flag leaf photosynthetic rate per unit area by 18% and chlorophyll A content by 23%

mechanism: TaARF4-A regulates TaHB33 expression (auxin-responsive pathway to height regulation).
evidence_sentence: TaARF4-A was shown to regulate HB33 expression which negatively responded to ABA, and reduced root length and plant height by repressing expression of Gretchen Hagen 3 (GH3) genes

mechanism: TaHB33 represses TaGH3 expression; TaGH3 conjugates auxin (reducing active auxin).
evidence_sentence: HB33 expression which negatively responded to ABA, and reduced root length and plant height by repressing expression of Gretchen Hagen 3 (GH3) genes

mechanism: GH3 enzymes conjugate IAA with amino acids, reducing active auxin pool.
evidence_sentence: repressing expression of Gretchen Hagen 3 (GH3) genes

mechanism: DELLA N-terminus uniquely controls seed dormancy; ovg suppressor alleles enhance dormancy when retaining N-terminal function.
evidence_sentence: Semi-dwarf ovg alleles increased dormancy, which correlated with improved pre-harvest sprouting resistance

mechanism: GA-insensitive Rht-B1b/Rht-D1b shorten coleoptile; GA-responsive dwarfs (Rht18/24) do NOT.
evidence_sentence: no shortening of the coleoptile in plants carrying the Rht18 allele, contrasting sharply with the coleoptile reduction observed in Rht-D1b lines

mechanism: TaZnF-B activates BR signalling; znf loss reduces plant height ~8 cm without yield penalty; znf+rht1 double restores WT height with larger grains.
evidence_sentence: znf-bb (single): Shorter (8.4 cm); Slight reduction; no pleiotropy

mechanism: Dwarfing DELLA alleles reduce anther length up to 20% (double Rht1+Rht2 combinations).
evidence_sentence: Decreases up to 20.2% in anther length were documented with the double gene combination

mechanism: TEOSINTE BRANCHED 1 regulates stem internode length; review-level evidence in wheat.
evidence_sentence: TEOSINTE BRANCHED 1 | Regulates height and stem internode length

mechanism: DELLA sequesters PIF transcription factors to suppress cell-elongation genes; accumulated Rht-B1c constitutively blocks PIFs.
evidence_sentence: The accumulated mutant protein constitutively blocks phytochrome-interacting factor activity, suppressing cell elongation genes

mechanism: PIFs activate cell elongation genes; when sequestered by DELLA, stem elongation decreases.
evidence_sentence: constitutively blocks phytochrome-interacting factor activity, suppressing cell elongation genes

mechanism: Red/far-red light via phytochromes targets PIFs for degradation; low light de-represses elongation.
evidence_sentence: phytochrome-interacting factor activity, suppressing cell elongation genes

mechanism: DELLA physically binds GRF4, inhibiting its growth/N-assimilation output; antagonistic balance.
evidence_sentence: physical interactions of the GROWTH-REGULATING FACTOR 4 (GRF4) transcription factor and the growth inhibitor DELLA confer homeostatic co-regulation

mechanism: ent-copalyl diphosphate synthase catalyses the first committed step of GA biosynthesis (GGPP -> ent-CPP); upstream of TaGA20ox/TaGA3ox.
evidence_sentence: The TaCPS, TaKS, TaKO, and TaKAO genes, all of which are involved in catalyzing the earlier steps of GA biosynthesis, were cloned from Chinese Spring wheat

mechanism: ent-kaurene synthase converts ent-CPP to ent-kaurene; second step of GA biosynthesis.
evidence_sentence: The TaCPS, TaKS, TaKO, and TaKAO genes, all of which are involved in catalyzing the earlier steps of GA biosynthesis, were cloned from Chinese Spring wheat

mechanism: ent-kaurene oxidase (P450) converts ent-kaurene to ent-kaurenoic acid in GA biosynthesis.
evidence_sentence: The TaCPS, TaKS, TaKO, and TaKAO genes, all of which are involved in catalyzing the earlier steps of GA biosynthesis, were cloned from Chinese Spring wheat

mechanism: ent-kaurenoic acid oxidase produces GA12 (entry to bioactive pathway); upstream of the rate-limiting TaGA20ox/TaGA3ox.
evidence_sentence: The TaCPS, TaKS, TaKO, and TaKAO genes, all of which are involved in catalyzing the earlier steps of GA biosynthesis, were cloned from Chinese Spring wheat

mechanism: Rht18 semidwarf allele confers ~4-fold higher expression of TaGA2ox-A9, raising GA catabolism and lowering bioactive GA1.
evidence_sentence: Expression of GA2oxA9 was ~4-fold higher in the Icaro and the M24 overgrowth mutant compared to the tall parent Anhinga

mechanism: Rht24b allele drives higher expression of TaGA2ox-A9 in stems, reducing bioactive GA at jointing stage; causal variant of Rht24 locus.
evidence_sentence: Rht24b conferred higher expression of TaGA2ox-A9 in stems, leading to a reduction of bioactive GA in stems

mechanism: Rht12b promoter SNP-390(C/A) increases TaGA2oxA13 expression, catabolising GA12 to inactive GA110 and reducing bioactive GA pool.
evidence_sentence: Rht12b conferred higher expression level of TaGA2oxA13, and transient activation assays defined SNP-390(C/A) in the promoter of TaGA2oxA13 as the causal variation

mechanism: TaDWF4 (CYP90B) catalyses the 22-alpha-hydroxylation of campesterol, the rate-limiting branch-point of BR biosynthesis.
evidence_sentence: Manipulating levels of DWF4/CYP90B, the rate-limiting step in the BR synthesis pathway, has been shown to increase yield

mechanism: TaSERK (BAK1 co-receptor family, SERK1-5) heterodimerises with TaBRI1 at the plasma membrane, required for BR perception and kinase activation.
evidence_sentence: TaBRI1 heterodimerizes with the TaSERKs viz. TaSERK1, TaSERK2, TaSERK3, TaSERK4 and TaSERK5

mechanism: TaERF-A1 (AP2/ERF TF) directly binds GCC-box in TaPIF4 promoter to activate TaPIF4 expression (EMSA, ChIP, transient expression).
evidence_sentence: TaERF-A1 directly associates with the promoter of TaPIF4 to activate its expression

mechanism: TaERF-A1 positively regulates stem elongation via TaPIF4; natural JD6 variant (F178S) destabilises protein and causes semi-dwarfing.
evidence_sentence: TaERF-A1 acts as a positive regulator of wheat stem elongation and regulates plant height and spike length

mechanism: Dominant Ppd-D1a (photoperiod-insensitive) allele shortens each internode by ~10%, reducing plant height; effect is weaker than Rht12 but significant across genetic backgrounds.
evidence_sentence: the dominant Ppd-D1a allele decreases plant height in both the dwarf and tall lines, though its effect is much weaker than that of Rht12

mechanism: Ppd-1 is a pseudo-response regulator whose expression is entrained by day-length; long-day induces Ppd-1 driving early flowering and shortened stem-elongation window.
evidence_sentence: Flowering time in wheat and barley is known to be modified by mutations in the Photoperiod-1 (Ppd-1) gene

mechanism: TaOSCA1.4 is a transmembrane hyperosmolality-gated Ca2+ channel; RNAi knockdown reduces plant height by 11-16% via Ca2+-signaling route independent of GA-DELLA.
evidence_sentence: the average PHs of the wild-type (WT), RNAi lines 1 (Ri-1) and 2 (Ri-2) were 94.6, 83.6 and 79.2 cm, respectively

mechanism: Nitrate signalling activates GA metabolism gene expression, raising bioactive GA content; canonical node through which nitrogen promotes stem elongation.
evidence_sentence: nitrate reduces the abundance of DELLAs by increasing GA contents through activation of GA metabolism gene expression

mechanism: Nitrate promotes GA-mediated destabilisation of DELLA proteins (including Rht-B1 homeolog), relieving growth repression under sufficient N.
evidence_sentence: the growth restraint conferred by nitrate deficiency is partially rescued in global-DELLA mutant
